content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (like ICM in autonomous agents) can be repurposed to actively prioritize which new memory traces an active learning agent should retain and fine-tune on, rather than retaining all encountered data or selecting based only on task predictive uncertainty. This creates a self-sustaining memory growth cycle where the agent’s own innate curiosity drives it to expand its memory with surprising, high-value information that improves generalizable future performance.
